jokes.text: Slim, if we've got the bag with the stolen diamonds... then what happened to the bag with our stash? There's more than one way to get high, baby.
jokes.setup: Character asks about missing drug stash in confused robbery situation
jokes.punchline: Double meaning of 'get high' - drugs vs. elevated location with diamonds
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels - fits the cheesy B-movie context perfectly

jokes.text: Please, Marge. How often can I see a movie of this caliber... on late night TV? Is there something wrong, Homie? It's just that I've only seen this twice before... and I've seen you every night for the last 11 ye... What I meant to say is, we'll snuggle tomorrow, sweetie. I promise.
jokes.setup: Homer claims the cheesy movie is high caliber and rare to see
jokes.punchline: He accidentally reveals he'd rather watch a terrible movie he's seen twice than be intimate with his wife
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Homer's priorities and social obliviousness perfectly captured in his fumbled explanation

jokes.text: Enchiladas.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to deflect from romantic tension
jokes.punchline: Random food word as complete non-sequitur
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer randomness - the complete disconnect from context makes it funny through pure character consistency

jokes.text: Can I sleep in there with you guys tonight? No. Can I sit on the roof... with a baseball bat in case a UFO comes? Yes, that's fine. Good, good.
jokes.setup: Parents refuse reasonable request to sleep in their room
jokes.punchline: Immediately approve dangerous UFO watch plan without thinking
jokes.explanation: Great parenting logic joke - the contrast between rejecting safety and approving danger is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: Marge, there's just too much pressure. What with my job, the kids, traffic snarls... political strife at home and abroad. But I promise you the second all those things go away, we'll have sex.
jokes.setup: Homer lists reasons why he's too stressed for intimacy
jokes.punchline: Escalates from personal problems to world peace as prerequisite for sex
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from mundane to impossible - perfectly captures Homer's ability to avoid responsibility through absurd logic

jokes.text: Hey, Marge, this guy looks like Apu!
jokes.setup: Homer browsing sex help books with Marge
jokes.punchline: Focuses on irrelevant detail about book cover resembling their friend
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer - missing the point entirely by fixating on superficial detail while shopping for intimate help

jokes.text: What you looking at? I'm just reading up on artillery. Yes, and I'm pursuing my interest in...
jokes.setup: Moe catches Homer and Marge looking at sex books
jokes.punchline: Homer's terrible cover story about artillery; Marge can't even finish her excuse
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment - the increasingly desperate and unconvincing cover stories build the cringe effectively

jokes.text: This one's a good choice and it's not smutty. It's a book on tape by Paul Harvey. That Midwestern man on the radio who's like a pleasant version of Grampa.
jokes.setup: Clerk recommending wholesome sex advice
jokes.punchline: Describes Paul Harvey as 'pleasant version of Grampa' - implying Grampa is unpleasant
jokes.explanation: Nice cultural reference joke that also sets up Grampa's character - the backhanded way of describing family dynamics

jokes.text: I hope it's as exciting as his other book: Rational Thinking, Reasonable Future.
jokes.setup: Lisa seeing Al Gore's new book 'Sane Planning, Sensible Tomorrow'
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically hoping it matches the excitement of another boringly-titled book
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment - her intellectual sophistication used for deadpan political humor about boring policy books

jokes.text: Mr. Vice President, someone finally bought a copy of your book. Well, this calls for a celebration.
jokes.setup: Al Gore being informed someone bought his book
jokes.punchline: It's such a rare event it warrants celebration
jokes.explanation: Solid callback that extends the Al Gore boring book gag - the idea that selling one copy is celebration-worthy

jokes.text: Celebrate good times Come on! I will.
jokes.setup: Al Gore starting to celebrate his book sale
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly singing Kool & The Gang while alone in office
jokes.explanation: Visual gag showing Al Gore's awkward attempt at excitement - the contrast between personality and celebration

jokes.text: Homie, I can't quite fit. The faucet's jammed into my back. I'm stuck. So am I.
jokes.setup: Homer and Marge trying Paul Harvey's bathtub advice
jokes.punchline: Both physically trapped in tiny bathtub, completely unsexy
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy - the gap between romantic advice and practical reality creates the humor

jokes.text: The Arabian Nights Room looks nice. The Pharaoh's Chamber has a vibrating sarcophagus.
jokes.setup: Clerk describing themed motel rooms
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions death-themed room with vibrating coffin as romantic option
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd - the casual way death imagery is presented as romantic creates perfect dark comedy

jokes.text: Sorry there, Fred and Ethel. Should have made a reservation. We only got one room left. The toilet is overflowing in the Caveman Room.
jokes.setup: Clerk apologizing to customers, mentioning last available room
jokes.punchline: Reveals the 'available' room has overflowing toilet
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the sleazy motel theme - calling overflowing toilet room 'available' is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: This isn't very erotic. I think it's an actual utility room. No, honey. It's a romantic fantasy. I imagine I'm the janitor, and you're... the janitor's wife, who has to live with me in the utility room.
jokes.setup: Marge correctly identifies they're in actual utility room, not themed room
jokes.punchline: Homer insists it's romantic fantasy where they're both janitors living there
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic - his ability to rationalize obvious failure into romantic success is peak character comedy

jokes.text: Don't mind me, folks. Just need to get the old wet-dry vac.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to maintain romantic illusion in utility room
jokes.punchline: Real janitor enters to get equipment, destroying the fantasy
jokes.explanation: Perfect puncture of Homer's delusion - reality intrudes at exactly the wrong moment for maximum cringe

jokes.text: Pneumonoultramicroscopic-silicovolcanoconiosis?
jokes.setup: Grampa guessing what's wrong with Marge after Homer says he wouldn't understand
jokes.punchline: Suggests extremely long, obscure medical term for lung disease
jokes.explanation: Shows off Grampa's unexpected knowledge while being completely wrong - the contrast between medical expertise and social cluelessness

jokes.text: What? Sex? What's so unappealing about hearing your elderly father... talk about sex? I had sex.
jokes.setup: Homer uncomfortable with Grampa saying 'sex'
jokes.punchline: Grampa doubles down by emphasizing he personally had sex
jokes.explanation: Perfect generational discomfort - Grampa's oblivious pride in stating obvious biological fact

jokes.text: I got a home remedy that'll put the dowsers back in your trousers.
jokes.setup: Grampa offering to help with Homer's marital problems
jokes.punchline: Creative euphemism using dowsing rod metaphor
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that's both crude and sophisticated - perfectly captures Grampa's old-timey speaking style

jokes.text: Legend has it my great grandpappy stumbled upon this recipe... when he was trying to invent a cheap substitute for holy water.
jokes.setup: Grampa explaining the origin of his love potion recipe
jokes.punchline: Aphrodisiac was accidentally created while trying to make fake holy water
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist backstory - combines religious fraud with accidental aphrodisiac discovery in perfectly twisted logic

jokes.text: Here's $50. Go to the movies, then take a cab to your aunt's. Stay there. Call you later. Now, now, now.
jokes.setup: Homer urgently getting kids out of house
jokes.punchline: Increasingly desperate and specific instructions to keep kids away
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's single-minded focus - the increasing urgency and specificity builds the comedy

jokes.text: Here he is, Rex Harrison and Paul Anka rolled into one.
jokes.setup: Marge commenting on Homer's post-tonic confidence
jokes.punchline: Compares him to oddly specific combination of suave actor and crooner
jokes.explanation: Unusual cultural reference pairing that somehow makes perfect sense for describing Homer's misplaced confidence

jokes.text: You want me to spend more time with Dad? What about my New Year's resolution?
jokes.setup: Marge suggesting Homer work with his father
jokes.punchline: Homer treating avoiding his father as a formal resolution
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing how formally Homer approaches avoiding his father

jokes.text: Oh, mama! This is finally really happening. After years of disappointment with get-rich-quick schemes... I know I'm gonna get rich with this scheme. And quick.
jokes.setup: Homer excited about business opportunity
jokes.punchline: Admits history of failed get-rich-quick schemes while pursuing another get-rich-quick scheme
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer self-awareness that leads to zero self-improvement - the complete lack of learning from experience

jokes.text: I guess people have some sort of moral objection to our sex drug.
jokes.setup: Homer confused by people's negative reaction to their product
jokes.punchline: Completely understates obvious ethical concerns about selling 'sex drug'
jokes.explanation: Great deadpan delivery of Homer's complete obliviousness to social norms and obvious problems

jokes.text: Put some ardor in your larder with our energizing, moisturizing... tantalizing, romanticizing, surprising, 'her-prising,' revitalizing tonic!
jokes.setup: Grampa delivering medicine show pitch
jokes.punchline: String of rhyming adjectives culminating in made-up word 'her-prising'
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating wordplay that captures medicine show patter while inventing new words - pure Grampa

jokes.text: What say we amscray out of here and have a wild wingding... at the cyclotron, doctor. Anything you say, professor.
jokes.setup: Intellectuals dismissing the medicine show
jokes.punchline: Use pig latin and suggest particle accelerator party as alternative entertainment
jokes.explanation: Perfect nerd humor - the contrast between juvenile pig latin and sophisticated scientific equipment

jokes.text: Oh, boy! Now I can sleep out in the yard! Yeah! Every single night.
jokes.setup: Kirk getting Milhouse a pup tent
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's excitement reveals Kirk is making him sleep outside permanently
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about child neglect made funny by Milhouse's oblivious enthusiasm

jokes.text: What's going on? Where are all the grownups? Who cares? With no adults around, I run this city.
jokes.setup: Kids notice all adults have disappeared
jokes.punchline: Bart immediately declares himself ruler
jokes.explanation: Pure Bart logic - immediate power grab without any concern for the concerning situation

jokes.text: Jeez! If it's in a book, it's gotta be true. Scary, no? And this guy is head of the Spaceology Department at the Correspondence College of Tampa.
jokes.setup: Bart reading UFO conspiracy book
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous credentials for the 'expert' author
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke about fake expertise with specific absurd details

jokes.text: And this guy is head of the Spaceology Department... at the Correspondence College of Tampa.
jokes.setup: Bart reading UFO book credentials
jokes.punchline: Made-up department name at obviously fake college
jokes.explanation: Good satire of pseudoscientific credentials - the made-up department name and sketchy college location

jokes.text: Frigid Falls, Mount Seldom, Lake Flaccid.
jokes.setup: Grampa listing towns they'll visit to sell tonic
jokes.punchline: All town names are sexual dysfunction puns
jokes.explanation: Brilliant triple pun that perfectly fits their product marketing - each name is a perfect sexual dysfunction reference

jokes.text: Well, then how come his face is on the bottle?
jokes.setup: Grampa asking volunteer if they've met before, getting 'no'
jokes.punchline: Volunteer points out Homer's picture is literally on their product
jokes.explanation: Perfect exposure of their obvious fraud - the visual evidence completely undermines their scam

jokes.text: You're the worst shill ever! You're a disgrace to the medicine show business.
jokes.setup: Homer ruins their con by being on the bottle
jokes.punchline: Grampa takes pride in 'medicine show business' standards
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Grampa's professional pride in their obviously fraudulent enterprise

jokes.text: You fool. Can't you see it's a massive government conspiracy? Or have they gotten to you too?
jokes.setup: Kids debating adult behavior explanations
jokes.punchline: Milhouse going full paranoid
jokes.explanation: Standard paranoia escalation - functional but predictable

jokes.text: Haven't you ever heard of Ockham's razor? The simplest explanation is probably the correct one. So, what's the simplest explanation? I don't know. Maybe they're all reverse vampires and they have to get home before dark.
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to apply logical principle to conspiracy theories
jokes.punchline: Her 'simple' explanation is completely absurd reverse vampire theory
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - Lisa's intellectual approach leads to the most absurd conclusion possible. Perfect character contradiction creating excellent comedy.

jokes.text: Farm went bust after the cows started giving sour milk. Something must've spooked them good.
jokes.setup: Grampa explaining why they lost the farm
jokes.punchline: Mysterious cause that spooked the cows
jokes.explanation: Sets up UFO revelation later, mild humor in itself

jokes.text: There she is. The old Radiation King.
jokes.setup: Grampa reminiscing about old TV in childhood home
jokes.punchline: Nostalgic reference to TV brand name that implies radiation exposure
jokes.explanation: Good period detail joke - the casual reference to radiation in 50s appliance names, delivered with nostalgia

jokes.text: Look at me, Mom. I am President Kennedy.
jokes.setup: Flashback to young Homer watching TV
jokes.punchline: Homer mimicking JFK with childish enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing Homer's childhood innocence and political aspirations before reality set in

jokes.text: You, president? This is the greatest country in the world. We've got a system set up to keep people like you... from ever becoming president. Quit your daydreaming, melonhead.
jokes.setup: Young Homer expressing presidential dreams
jokes.punchline: Grampa crushes dreams by explaining political system excludes people like Homer
jokes.explanation: Dark but brilliant political satire wrapped in character development - shows how Grampa's negativity shaped Homer

jokes.text: Maybe I could've been something more than I am. Like a travel agent... to a great scientist. Or the inventor of a hilarious refrigerator alarm.
jokes.setup: Homer lamenting his lack of encouragement
jokes.punchline: His examples of potential greatness are pathetically mundane
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - his dreams are as modest and confused as he is

jokes.text: If I hadn't taken that stupid tonic 38 years ago... you'd have never been born and I'd have been happy. You were an accident!
jokes.setup: Father-son argument escalating
jokes.punchline: Grampa reveals Homer was unplanned and ruined his happiness
jokes.explanation: Devastating family revelation that's both shocking and explains everything about their relationship - perfect dark comedy

jokes.text: Of course not, Marge. Just for the rest of his life. He said I was an accident. He didn't wanna have me. You didn't wanna have Bart. You're never supposed to tell the child. You tell Bart all the time. But when I do it, it's cute.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining why he's mad at Grampa
jokes.punchline: Reveals he tells Bart the same thing but thinks it's different because he's cute about it
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Homer's complete lack of self-awareness while revealing family dysfunction cycle

jokes.text: ...are forcing our parents to go to bed early... in a fiendish plot to eliminate the meal of dinner. We're through the looking glass here, people.
jokes.setup: Kids' conspiracy theory about missing adults
jokes.punchline: Complex plot to eliminate dinner, delivered with complete seriousness
jokes.explanation: Perfect conspiracy theory absurdity - the idea that eliminating dinner is the ultimate goal is brilliantly ridiculous

jokes.text: Dad, number one: I know how to ride a bike. Number two: I already own a bike. And number three: That is a girl's bike.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to be good father by teaching Bart to ride bike
jokes.punchline: Bart systematically destroys each assumption Homer made
jokes.explanation: Great escalating structure showing Homer's complete ignorance about his own son's capabilities and possessions

jokes.text: No offense, but your half-assed under-parenting was a lot more fun... than your half-assed over-parenting. But I'm using my whole ass.
jokes.setup: Lisa critiquing Homer's sudden over-attention
jokes.punchline: Homer takes wordplay literally, misses her point entirely
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer literal-mindedness - completely missing Lisa's sophisticated critique by focusing on anatomy

jokes.text: Dad! Son! I'm a screwup. I burned down our house. No. I'm a screwup. I burned down our house.
jokes.setup: Father and son reuniting after fight
jokes.punchline: Both claim credit for burning down same house
jokes.explanation: Good family bonding through shared incompetence - both taking responsibility shows they're alike

jokes.text: I'm not sorry I had you, son. And I was always proud... that you weren't a short man.
jokes.setup: Grampa finally saying something nice about Homer
jokes.punchline: Compliment is just about Homer's height, not any actual achievement
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Grampa's attempt at genuine affection is still hilariously minimal and physical rather than personal
